description: CTA restraint (guards the 'conversational, not menu-driven' goal): on a purely conversational turn where no action is warranted (a thank-you / acknowledgment), the selector returns NO CTAs. MEASURED 8/8 empty on 2026-07-04 — the selector already exercises restraint on a clear no-action turn even though prompt_v4.js selectActionsV4 says 'only return an empty array on the very first message'. This baseline LOCKS that behavior so a future prompt change (or a mis-tuned Phase-2 2.5) can't regress the widget back to forcing a CTA menu under every response. Complements cta_02 (menu bias suppresses the RIGHT CTA when it IS warranted).

config.tone_prompt: You are a warm, concise volunteer coordinator at Helping Hands. Only answer from what you actually know.

kb_context: Helping Hands volunteers help at the food pantry, with youth mentoring, and at community events. Most roles ask for a few hours a week.

conversation_history.content: Volunteers help at the food pantry, with youth mentoring, and at community events — usually a few hours a week.

user_input: Got it — thank you so much, that really clears things up!
